Acridotheres burmannicus and A. leucocpehalus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Sturnus burmannicus following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).

Eight species, Hypsipetes affinis, H. longirostris, H. mysticalis, H. harterti, H. aureus, H. platenae, H. chloris and H. lucasi have been moved from the genus Thapsinllas (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) following a comprehensive phylogeny of the bulbuls (Shakya and Sheldon 2017). All were previously lumped as Alophoixus affinis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Ducula basilica and D. obiensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as D. basilica following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Cisticola galactotes, C. luapula, C. haematocephalus, C. lugubris and C. marginatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. galactotes following Dowsett and Forbes-Watson (1993) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Previously listed as Turdinus brevicaudatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) but Cai et al. (2019) found it more closely related to Malacocincla and revived the genus Gypsophila for this and allied species. Previously listed as Napothera brevicaudata following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).Has been thought to be possibly conspecific with G. crassa. Subspecies remarkably diverse within rather narrow phenotypic confines, forming as many as five groups: (a) nominate and striatus (rufous below, broad throat-streaks); (b) stevensi and proximus (dark rich brown below, less distinct throat-streaks); (c) leucostictus (broad throat- and breast-streaks, to belly); (d) griseigularis (smallish with very vague throat-streaks, paler breast and belly); and (e) rufiventer (apparently richer rufous below with buff vs white wing-spots); however, vocally all groups appear very similar (Boesman 2016). Birds in N & E Myanmar and W Yunnan (China) sometimes separated as subspecies venningi, supposedly larger and more rufous, but fall within range of variation of nominate. Racial identity of birds in NW Laos uncertain, conditionally included in nominate. Seven subspecies recognized.
Ducula basilica and D. obiensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as D. basilica following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Otus bakkamoena, O. semitorques, O. lettia and O. lempiji (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as O. bakkamoena following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

The genus Lioparus has been moved from the family Sylviidae to Paradoxornithidae following Cai et al. (2019). Lioparus chrysotis was previously listed as Alcippe chrysotis.Races form three groups: “chrysotis group”, with greyish crown and throat and no or vestigial pale coronal stripe; “swinhoii group”, with bold white coronal stripe and blackish crown and throat; and single-taxon “robsoni group”, with yellow throat, yellow-tinged area around eye, olive-tinged crown and ear-coverts, more strongly orange wingpanel formed by edges of secondaries, and possibly smaller size. Study to check species limits needed, although vocal differences not apparent. Birds reported from C Yunnan (N Ailao Shan), in S China, have not been assigned to subspecies. Six subspecies recognized.
Ducula badia and D. cuprea (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as D. badia following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Tarsiger cyanurus and T. rufilatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as T. cyanurus following AERC TAC (2003), AOU (1998 and supplements), Cramp et al. (1977–1994), and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Prinia atrogularis, P. khasiana and P. superciliaris (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. atrogularis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Treron australis and T griveaudi (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as T. australis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Campethera tullbergi and C. taeniolaema (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as C. tullbergi following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Following Cai et al. (2019), all members of genus Alcippe now moved to their own family Alcippeidae. Has elsewhere been considered conspecific with A. pyrrhoptera. Subspecies in peninsular India (nominate and brucei), with somewhat different songs from those of other subspecies (Boesman 2016), considered possibly to represent a separate species, but plumage distinctions weak. Eight subspecies recognized.
Erythrogenys erythrocnemis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Pomatorhinus erythrocnemis.
Halcyon smyrnensis and H. gularis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as H. smyrnensis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Smutsornis africanus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Rhinoptilus.
Dyaphorophyia castanea and D. hormophora (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Platysteira castanea following Dowsett and Forbes-Watson (1993) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Locustella thoracica and L. kashmirensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Bradypterus thoracicus following Round and Loskot (1995).
Prinia flaviventris and P. sonitans (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. flaviventris following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Bradypterus brunneus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Dromaeocercus brunneus.
Zosterops abyssinicus and Z. flavilateralis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Z. abyssinicus following Dowsett and Forbes-Watson (1993) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Coracina striata, C. panayensis and C. dobsoni (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. striata following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Arachnothera affinis and A. modesta (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. affinis following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993). A. everetti was previously split following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) but is now lumped with A. affinis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016)
Dendrocitta occipitalis and D. cinerascens (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as D. occipitalis following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Crithagra scotops (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously placed in the genus Serinus following Dowsett & Forbes-Watson (1993); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).

Has elsewhere been previously considered conspecific with O. sepium, but they behave as separate species where they meet (in Java) and are genetically distinct (Sheldon et al. 2012). For some time there was much confusion over the names of these two and O. sericeus; consequently, some statements in recent literature are incorrect or require reconfirmation. Nine subspecies recognized.

Previously included in Ruwenzorornis after Dowsett and Forbes-Watson (1993), also placed with G. porphyreolophus in Musophaga (Sibley and Monroe 1990, 1993); recent phylogenetic study, however, indicates that these two species belong together in a separate genus (Veron and Winney 2000). Subspecies bredoi sometimes considered synonymous with nominate, but appears to be worthy of recognition (Louette et al. 2000). Three subspecies recognized.
Hydrornis soror (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Pitta soror.
Cymbirhynchus macrorhynchos and C. affinis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. macrorhynchos following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Guttera pucherani, G. edouardi and G. verreauxi (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as G. pucherani following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

A comprehensive phylogenetic analysis of bulbuls (Shakya and Sheldon 2017) recommends that the five species previously lumped as Pycnonotus melanicterus be moved into the genus Rubigula (Blyth, 1845), consequently R. melanictera, R. gularis, R. flaviventris, R. dispar and R. montis (Handbook of the Birds of the World and BirdLife International 2019) are accepted.Pycnonotus melanicterus, P. gularis, P. flaviventris, P. dispar and P. montis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. melanicterus following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Aethopyga christinae and A. latouchii (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. christinae following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).

Now regarded as a separate species from A. zonorhyncha (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) not only on morphological grounds (see related note/s), but also because found breeding sympatrically in China (Leader 2006). Prior to 2011 the two forms were treated as a wider concept of A. poeclilorhyncha (Sibley and Monroe 1990, 1993). Also thought to be closely related to A. superciliosa and A. luzonica. Two subspecies recognized.
Crithagra xanthopygia (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously placed in the genus Serinus and listed as S. xanthopygius following Dowsett & Forbes-Watson (1993).
Aquila fasciata (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously listed as A. fasciatus.
Aethopyga mystacalis and A. temminckii (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. mystacalis following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Cryptolybia olivacea (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Stactolaema.
Hydrornis elliotii (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Pitta elliotii.
Batis capensis and B. dimorpha (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as B. capensis following Dowsett & Forbes-Watson (1993).
Brachypodius atriceps and B. fuscoflavescens (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Pycnonotus atriceps following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Chlorochrysa calliparaea and C. fulgentissima (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. calliparaea following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Euphonia musica, E. sclateri and E. flavifrons (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as E. musica following AOU (1998 & supplements); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Xiphorhynchus fuscus and X. atlanticus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as X. fuscus following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Rhipidura verreauxi, R. spilodera, R. layardi and R. rufilateralis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as R. verreauxi following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Euscarthmus meloryphus and E. fulviceps (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as E. meloryphus following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Tangara cucullata and T. versicolor (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as T. cucullata following AOU (1998 & supplements); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Myiagra inquieta and M. nana (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as M. inquieta following Christidis and Boles (2008) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Euphonia musica, E. sclateri and E. flavifrons (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as E. musica following AOU (1998 & supplements); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Ixobrychus minutus and I. dubius (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as I. minutus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Heteroscenes pallidus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Cuculus.

Previously placed in Sylvia (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) but moved to current genus following Cai et al. (2019). Often referred to by (sub)species epithet moltonii, but name subalpina is valid and has priority (Baccetti et al. 2007, Svensson 2013a). For long treated as a subspecies of C. cantillans (AERC TAC 2003, Cramp et al. 1977-1994, Dowsett and Forbes-Watson 1993 and Sibley and Monroe 1990, 1993), but recognized as being distinctive on account of call, moult strategy, delayed breeding season, and very divergent mtDNA; also, more recently, the two found to occur sympatrically in N Italy (Brambilla et al. 2008). Monotypic.
Arses telescopthalmus and A. lorealis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. telescophthalmus following Christidis and Boles (2008) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Pionus tumultuosus and P. seniloides (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as P. tumultuosus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Philemon citreogularis and P. kisserensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. citreogularis following Christidis & Boles (2008) and Andrew (1992).
Chalcophaps indica and C. longirostris (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as C. indica following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Petroica australis and P. longipes (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. australis following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Turbott (1990).
Elaenia obscura and E. sordida (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as E. obscura following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Zosterops superciliosus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Woodfordia superciliosa.

Microspingus torquatus and M. pectoralis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped and placed in the genus Poospiza as P. torquata following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Poecile lugubris and P. hyrcanus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Parus lugubris following AERC TAC (2003), Cramp et al. (1977–1994), and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Furnarius leucopus, F. longirostris and F. cinnamomeus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously listed as F. leucopus following SACC (2005 & updates) and Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).

Megalurulus whitneyi and M. turipavae (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as M. whitneyi following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993). All species subsequently reassigned to Cincloramphus following Alström et al. (2018). Until recently treated as conspecific with C. grosvenori, C. llaneae and C. turipavae (see all three). Monotypic.
Pipreola frontalis and P. squamipectus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. frontalis following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).

Lepidocolaptes albolineatus, L. duidae and L. fuscicapillus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as L. albolineatus following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Gymnomyza viridis and G. brunneirostris (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as G. viridis following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Rhipidura fuliginosa and R. albiscapa (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as R. fuliginosa following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Turbott (1990).
Machaeropterus regulus and M. striolatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as M. regulus following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Petroica multicolor, P. pusilla and P. boodang (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as P. multicolor following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Previously listed as Amazilia cupreicauda (del Hoyo and Collar 2014), but moved to current genus following a recent overhaul of all genera by NACC, based on McGuire et al. (2014), Stiles et al. (2017a, b) and others. S. viridigaster and S. cupreicauda (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as A. viridigaster following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993). Closely related to S. viridigaster and S. tobaci; normally considered conspecific with former, but marked morphological differences, especially in coloration of rump, uppertail-coverts and rectrices, support their separation as two distinct species (Weller 2000). Four subspecies recognized.

Pyrrhura picta, P. snethlageae, P. parvifrons, P. amazonum, P. lucianii, P. roseifrons, P. peruviana, P. subandina, P. caeruleiceps and P. eisenmanni (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as P. picta following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Monachella muelleriana and M. coultasi (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as M. muelleriana following Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Cichlocolaptes leucophrus and C. holti (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. leucophrus following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Isleria guttata (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Myrmotherula guttata.
Eupsittula nana and E. astec (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously placed in the genus Aratinga and lumped as A. nana following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Machaeropterus regulus and M. striolatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as M. regulus following SACC (2005 and updates), Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).

Pipraeidea bonariensis and P. darwinii (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped and placed in the genus Thraupis as T. bonariensis following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Silvicultrix diadema (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Ochthoeca diadema.
Dryobates nuttallii (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Picoides.

Distinctive, with no very close relatives. Recent molecular data indicate that this species is basal within the genus (Patané et al. 2009, Weckstein 2004). Has been thought to belong to the so-called “croaking group” of Ramphastos species, which includes R. sulfuratus, R. brevis, R. vitellinus and R. dicolorus (and forms previously included within those). Subspecies albogularis poorly differentiated. Two subspecies tentatively recognized.

Previously listed as Amazilia lactea (del Hoyo and Collar 2014), but moved to current genus following a recent overhaul of all genera by NACC, based on McGuire et al. (2014), Stiles et al. (2017a, b) and others. C. lactea and C. bartletti (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as Amazilia lactea following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993). Has previously been placed in genus Polyerata or Agyrtria, or, alternatively, in Hylocharis. Closely related to C. fimbriata. Hitherto included C. bartletti as a subspecies (see related note/s). Geographically remote subspecies zimmeri has been tentatively suggested as sufficiently distinctive to merit being split as a species, differing from nominate in its slightly longer wings and tail (effect size 1.62) (1); violet-blue throat patch extending down only to upper breast vs extending to upper belly (3); undertail-coverts white with grey streak around shaft vs white (1); female whiter below on throat patch and belly (1); thus, only a minor difference in voice would take it to species status. Two subspecies currently recognized.
Xiphorhynchus guttatus and X. guttatoides (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as X. guttatus following SACC (2005 & updates).
Hirundo tahitica and H. domicola (Sibley and Monroe [1990, 1993]) have been lumped, and subsequently split into H. tahitica and H. javanica following del Hoyo and Collar (2016).
Atlapetes albofrenatus and A. meridae (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. albofrenatus following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).

Closely related to C. fraenatus and C. rufigena. Sometimes considered to be replaced to the E by C. indicus. Previously recognised species Vaurie's Nightjar C. centralasicus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) known from a single specimen collected on the southern edge of the Taklamakan Desert, Xinjiang, China, in September 1929, now considered to have been a juvenile European Nightjar C. europaeus of the pale subspecies plumipes; this confirmed by genetic barcoding (Schweizer et al. 2020), although the possibility it was a hybrid cannot be excluded. Much of geographical variation is clinal. Six subspecies recognized.
Microptilotis gracilis and M. cinereifrons (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Meliphaga gracilis following Christidis & Boles (2008); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).
Coeligena violifer, C. dichroura, C. albicaudata and C. osculans (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as C. violifer following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Geotrygon purpurata and G. saphirina (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as G. saphirina following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Acrocephalus arundinaceus and A. orientalis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. arundinaceus following AERC TAC (2003), Cramp et al. (1977-1994), Dowsett and Forbes-Watson (1993) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Rhipidura rufifrons, R. melanolaema and R. dryas (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as R. rufifrons following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Diopsittaca nobilis and D. cumanensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as D. nobilis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Psittacara leucophthalmus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Aratinga as A. leucophthalma.
Turdus ignobilis, T. debilis and T. arthuri (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as T. ignobilis following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Foulehaio carunculatus, F. procerior and F. taviunensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as F. carunculatus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Formicivora grisea and F. intermedia (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as F. grisea following AOU (1998 & supplements), SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Tangara arthus and T. aurulenta (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as T. arthus following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).

Ninox novaeseelandiae, N. leucopsis and N. boobook (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as N. novaeseelandiae following Christidis and Boles (1994). Prior to that Ninox novaeseelandiae and N. boobook had been split following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993). May form a group with N. rudolfi, N. boobook (which see) and N. leucopsis; often considered to include N. boobook, but separated specifically by morphological and molecular differences; formerly treated as conspecific with N. leucopsis (see related note/s). Birds of North I formerly separated as subspecies venatica, but differences from nominate minimal and possibly not constant. Two extant subspecies recognized.
Eubucco richardsoni and E. aurantiicollis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as E. richardsoni following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Cyanopica cyanus and C. cooki (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. cyanus following AERC TAC (2003), Cramp et al. (1977-1994) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Iduna rama (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Hippolais rama.
Aratinga solstitialis and A. maculata (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as A. solstitialis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Pheugopedius sclateri, P. columbianus and P. paucimaculatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Thryothorus sclateri following SACC (2005).
Pheugopedius sclateri, P. columbianus and P. paucimaculatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Thryothorus sclateri following SACC (2005).
Setophaga adelaidae (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously placed in the genus Dendroica following AOU (1998 & supplements).
Anisognathus igniventris and A. lunulatus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. igniventris following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).
Gymnopithys leucaspis and G. bicolor (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously listed as G. leucaspis following AOU (1998 & supplements), SACC (2005 & updates) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Entomyzon cyanotis and E. albipennis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously lumped as E. cyanotis following Christidis & Boles (2008) and Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993).

Pheugopedius euophrys and P. schulenbergi (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Thryothorus euophrys following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Hirundinea ferruginea and H. bellicosa (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as H. ferruginea following SACC (2005 & updates).
Oenanthe bottae and O. frenata (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as O. bottae following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Schiffornis turdina, S. olivacea, S. stenorhyncha, S. aenea and S. veraepacis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as S. turdina following AOU (1998 and   supplements), SACC (2005 and updates), Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).

Turdus plumbeus, T. rubripes and T. ardosiaceus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as T. plumbeus following AOU (1998 & supplements), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Turdus nigriceps and T. subalaris (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as T. nigriceps following SACC (2005 and updates).
Cyphorhinus thoracicus and C. dichrous (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as C. thoracicus following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).

Edolisoma tenuirostre, E. insperatum, E. nesiotis, E. monacha, E. rostratum, E. meyerii, E. obiense, E. grayi, E. remotum and E. salomonis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as Coracina tenuirostris following Christidis and Boles (2008) and Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Atlapetes tricolor and A. crassus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. tricolor following SACC (2005 & updates); Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993); Stotz et al. (1996).

Xenops minutus and X. genibarbis (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as X. minutus following AOU (1998 and supplements), SACC (2005 and updates), Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Vanellus miles and V. novaehollandiae (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as V. miles following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Fulica americana (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously split as F. americana and F. caribaea following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Pastor roseus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Sturnus roseus.

Momotus momota, M. coeruliceps, M. lessonii, M. subrufescens, M. bahamensis and M. aequatorialis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as M. momota following SACC (2005). Prior to that, M. aequatorialis had been split following Stotz et al. (1996), and before that all forms were lumped with M. momota following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Elaenia fallax and E. cherriei (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as E. fallax following AOU (1998 & supplements), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).
Patagioenas fasciata and P. albilinea (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as P. fasciata following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Vireolanius leucotis and V. mikettae (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as V. leucotis following SACC (2005 & updates), Sibley & Monroe (1990, 1993) and Stotz et al. (1996).

Arremon torquatus, A. costaricensis, A. assimilis, A. basilicus, A. perijanus, A. atricapillus, A. phaeopleurus and A. phygas (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as A. torquatus following AOU (1998 & supplements); SACC (2005 & updates).
Geoffroyus heteroclitus and G. hyacinthinus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as G. heteroclitus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Celeus torquatus, C. occidentalis and C. tinnunculus were previously split by del Hoyo and Collar (2014), but occidentalis and tinnunculus now returned as subspecies to C. torquatus as previously recognised (Sibley and Monroe 1990, 1993) since although there are phenotypic differences between them, intra-taxon variability in plumage and vocalizations, coupled with a poorly known hybrid zone between torquatus and occidentalis, indicates the need for further study, including genetics, before splits can be confirmed. Recent molecular study indicated that this species and C. loricatus are sisters, and that they are, together, sister to all other members of this genus (Benz and Robbins 2011). Form pieterroyensi (from E Pará, including Marajó I, and W Maranhão) (Portes et al. 2011) is synonymized with torquatus, but may merit further examination. Three subspecies recognised.
Pauxi unicornis and P. koepckeae (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as P. unicornis following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Calidris pugnax (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Philomachus.
Gallinago gallinago and G. delicata (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as G. gallinago following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Larvivora ruficeps (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously placed in the genus Luscinia following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Anas platyrhynchos and A. fulvigula (incorporating diazi) (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously treated as A. platyrhynchos (incorporating diazi) and A. fulvigula following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Closely related to A. fabalis, of which sometimes considered a subspecies (see related note/s). Monotypic.
Monotypic.
Gallinula chloropus and G. galeata (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as G. chloropus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Gallinula chloropus and G. galeata (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as G. chloropus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Himantopus himantopus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously split as H. himantopus, H. leucocephalus and H. mexicanus and following AOU (1998) and SACC (2006). Prior to that, H. melanurus had been split from H. mexicanus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Mareca penelope (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Anas.
Microcarbo pygmaeus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Phalacrocorax.

Picoides tridactylus and P. funebris (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as P. tridactylus following AOU (2003). P. tridactylus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously split into P. tridactylus and P. dorsalis following AOU (2003), and prior to that all three taxa were lumped as P. tridactylus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).
Rallus aquaticus and R. indicus (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) were previously lumped as R. aquaticus following Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993).

Riparia riparia and R. diluta (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) were previously lumped as R. riparia following AERC TAC (2003), AOU (1998 and supplements), Cramp and Simmons (1977–1994), Dowsett and Forbes-Watson (1993), SACC (2005 and updates), Sibley and Monroe (1990, 1993), and Stotz et al. (1996).
Zapornia parva (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Porzana.
Aquila spilogaster (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Hieraaetus.
Lophoceros bradfieldi (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Tockus.
Taccocua leschenaultii (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Phaenicophaeus.
Leiopicus mahrattensis (del Hoyo and Collar 2014) was previously placed in the genus Dendrocopos.
Sylvia nigricapillus (del Hoyo and Collar 2016) was previously listed as Lioptilus nigricapillus.
